new_classes.definition_en: A whisky produced by distilling a fermented mash of malted barley with or without whole grains of other cereals (predominantly non-malted grains such as wheat or maize), distilled in a continuous still, distinguished from malt whisky which uses only malted barley in a pot still.
A grain whisky produced at a single distillery by distilling a fermented mash of malted barley with or without whole grains of other cereals, other than single malt whisky or blended malt whisky.
A whisky produced by blending two or more single grain whiskies from different distilleries.

A single grain whisky that is a Scotch whisky, produced at a single distillery in Scotland by distilling a fermented mash of malted barley with or without whole grains of other cereals, other than single malt Scotch whisky or blended malt Scotch whisky, presented in containers of 2 litres or less.

new_classes.label_en: Single Grain Scotch Whisky in containers > 2 L (CN 2208 30 69)
new_classes.definition_en: A single grain Scotch whisky presented in containers holding more than 2 litres, classified under CN 2208 30 69.

new_classes.label_en: Mixed Cereal Grain Mash (malted barley with other cereals)
new_classes.label_de: Gemischte Getreidemaische (gemälzte Gerste mit anderen Getreidearten)
new_classes.definition_en: A fermented mash consisting of malted barley with or without whole grains of other cereals (such as wheat or maize), used as the feedstock for grain whisky distillation.
A distillation process using a continuous (column or patent) still, as opposed to a pot still, typically used in grain whisky production.

coverage_explanation:
The axioms cover the key distinguishing criteria for CN 2208 30 69:
COVERED:
1. Single grain whisky identity: produced at a single distillery (SingleDistilleryOriginQuality), from mixed cereal grain mash (malted barley ± other cereals), via whisky distillation process.
2. Scotch whisky identity: Scotland origin quality.
3. Container size discriminator: large container (> 2 L) via eucn:LargeContainer — this is the primary criterion separating 2208 30 69 from 2208 30 61 (≤ 2 L).
4. Distinction from single malt and blended malt whisky (complement restrictions).
5. Minimum alcoholic strength 40% vol quality (a defining Scotch whisky criterion).
6. Blended grain whisky is introduced as a sibling class under GrainWhisky (though CN 2208 30 69 covers both single grain and blended grain Scotch whisky in large containers per the note heading; the legal note text focuses on single grain Scotch whisky definition).

GAPS / REDUCED SCORE:
- The legal note text is truncated and does not provide the full definition of blended grain Scotch whisky. The blended grain whisky large container class is not separately modelled for Scotch (BlendedGrainScotchWhiskyLargeContainer) because the note truncation makes it unclear whether 2208 30 69 also covers blended grain Scotch in large containers, though the heading '22083061 and 22083069' suggests both sizes are covered together. This reduces completeness slightly.
- The continuous still (column still) requirement for grain whisky production is modelled as a new class (ContinuousStillDistillationProcess) but not yet connected via a restriction on the main node class, as the legal text is truncated before confirming this detail explicitly.
- The maturation requirement (minimum 3 years in oak casks), which is part of Scotch whisky regulations, is not modelled as it is not stated in the provided legal text.
